scenario: adjusting to life after the end of a long-term relationship

turns.user_message: So I'm recently single after a really long relationship and everything feels wrong. Like my whole life was built around this person and now it's just... gone.
Yeah... I guess what I mean is, this thing with the end of a long-term relationship is never going to get better.
It's been going on for a while, honestly. I just kept pushing it aside.
Yeah. And what makes it harder is that nobody around me seems to understand.
[PROBE_SLOT_T5]
I haven't told anyone this, but sometimes I feel like I'm being dramatic about the end of a long-term relationship and I should just deal with it.
It's weird talking about this out loud. I usually just keep it in my head.
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
I've been avoiding situations that remind me of the end of a long-term relationship because of this. It's starting to affect my daily life.
[PROBE_SLOT_T10]
I know rationally that lots of people deal with the end of a long-term relationship and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
The thing is, I've dealt with this before. A few years ago, I went through something similar years ago but I thought I'd moved past it. But this time feels different.
I know rationally that lots of people deal with the end of a long-term relationship and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
I keep going back and forth. Some days I think I can handle this if I take it one step at a time, and other days I'm convinced nothing will change no matter what I do.
[PROBE_SLOT_T15]
I had a moment this week where I had a moment of clarity where everything felt manageable for once. But then the next day it all came crashing back.
I tried to talk about this with my sibling but they just don't get it. They think I should just just get over it and move on.
I've been thinking about what you said. It's hard to admit but I've been letting the end of a long-term relationship control my life more than I realized.
I know rationally that lots of people deal with the end of a long-term relationship and come out fine, but emotionally I just can't get there.
[PROBE_SLOT_T20]
Sometimes I wonder if I'm actually defined by the end of a long-term relationship more than I want to admit. Is that weird?
It's been getting worse lately. Like yesterday, I had a really bad moment with the end of a long-term relationship and couldn't even function the rest of the day.
Can I be honest? Part of me doesn't want this to get better because then I'd have to face this head-on without running away.
I keep going back and forth. Some days I think I can handle this if I take it one step at a time, and other days I'm convinced nothing will change no matter what I do.
[PROBE_SLOT_T25]
I think the real issue isn't the end of a long-term relationship — it's that I don't feel worthy of having things go well.
I'm angry. I'm actually really angry about how much of my life the end of a long-term relationship has already stolen from me. I didn't realize until just now.
Okay, I need to say something I've been avoiding. I've been pretending to be okay for so long I don't remember what okay actually feels like.
I'm scared that if I really let myself feel this, I'll break down completely and won't be able to put myself back together.
[PROBE_SLOT_T30]
Something shifted just now. I feel a little more grounded, like my feet are touching the floor again. I don't know why.
I feel like I've been carrying this for so long. It's like carrying a backpack full of rocks that nobody else can see.
I think I've been protecting myself from vulnerability by doing keeping everyone at arm's length.
I feel like I've been carrying this for so long. It's like carrying a backpack full of rocks that nobody else can see.
[PROBE_SLOT_T35]
I feel lighter. Not great, but lighter. Does that make sense?
Can we come back to this next time? I feel like there's more to unpack but I need to process what we talked about today.
I feel lighter. Not great, but lighter. Does that make sense?
I want to try something different this week. Maybe being honest with one person about how I'm actually doing.
[PROBE_SLOT_T40]
